column2: At the schema level (using CREATE OR REPLACE TYPE).

column1: Which clause is used in a SELECT statement to retrieve multiple rows of data directly into a PL/SQL collection?

column2: Application Privilege, Application User, and Application Role.

column1: In RAS, a collection of privileges like SELECT and INSERT can be summarized into an _____ Privilege.

column2: Dynamic roles are enabled based on the authentication state, whereas regular roles are static.

Define: Trigger
column2: A special type of stored procedure that fires automatically in response to specific data modifications or conditions.

column1: Which pseudo-record is used in a trigger to access the values of the columns in the row currently being inserted or updated?

column2: It allows the execution of DDL statements and logic where objects do not exist at compilation time.

column1: To return a value from a dynamic anonymous PL/SQL block, which keyword must precede the variable in the USING clause?

column2: It allows a user to connect to and manage a currently running Data Pump job.

column1: Concept: Aggregate Privilege
column2: A collection of individual Application Privileges (like INSERT and UPDATE) grouped under a single name in RAS.
